channel.title: My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content on My New Hugo Site

item.title: Hello World: Our First Article
item.description:
Welcome to our news site — this is a sample article created to show how the theme looks with real content.

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Integer nec odio. Praesent libero. Sed cursus ante dapibus diam.

item.title: Getting Started with Hugo
item.description:
Hugo is a fast and flexible static site generator built with Go. In this article, we’ll explore the fundamentals.

Why Choose Hugo?

Hugo stands out for its:

  • Blazing Speed: Builds thousands of pages in seconds
  • Simple Content Model: Just Markdown files in folders
  • Flexible Templating: Go templates for powerful layouts
  • Zero Dependencies: Single binary, no runtime required

Getting Started

  1. Download Hugo from gohugo.io
  2. Create a new site with hugo new site mysite
  3. Add content under content/
  4. Run hugo server to preview locally
  5. Deploy the public/ folder

That’s it! You’re ready to start building.

item.title: The Future of Web Development
item.description:
The web development landscape is constantly evolving. Here’s what’s shaping the future.

Key Trends

1. Edge Computing

Bringing computation closer to users for lower latency and better performance.

2. AI Integration

Machine learning models are becoming more accessible for web developers, enabling smarter applications.

3. Web Standards Evolution

New browser APIs continue to enhance what’s possible in the browser without external plugins.

4. Static Site Generators

SSGs like Hugo are gaining popularity for their performance and simplicity benefits.

item.title: Why Static Sites Matter
item.description:
Static sites are experiencing a renaissance. Let’s explore why they’re the right choice for many projects.

Performance First

Static sites are served as plain HTML files. No database queries, no server-side rendering delays—just pure speed. This translates to better user experience and improved SEO.

Security by Default

With no server-side logic or database, there’s simply less to attack. Your site is as secure as your hosting platform.

Simplicity

Developers love static sites because:

item.title: Accessibility Best Practices for Web
item.description:
Accessibility isn’t optional—it’s a fundamental aspect of good web design. Here’s how to get it right.

Core Principles

1. Perceivable

Information must be visible or hearable:

  • Provide alt text for images
  • Use sufficient color contrast
  • Don’t rely on color alone to convey information

2. Operable

Users must be able to interact with your site:

  • Keyboard navigation must work
  • Avoid auto-playing media
  • Provide skip links for navigation

3. Understandable

Content should be clear and predictable:

item.title: Editor
item.description: Jane writes about tech, policy and community events.
